headline: Inflation jumps as AI security and consumer tech reshape the agenda
summary: U.S. inflation rose at its fastest pace in nearly four years after a war-driven spike in gasoline and diesel prices, putting fresh pressure on households and the White House. In tech, a suspect was arrested after an alleged Molotov cocktail attack on Sam Altman’s home and threats at OpenAI, while debate intensified over Anthropic’s new Mythos model and its cybersecurity risks. Apple’s foldable iPhone also moved closer to production, underscoring how fast product bets are advancing even as safety concerns grow around AI.
why_it_matters: Energy costs, AI security, and big-device launches are converging into the next set of pressures on consumers, markets, and the tech industry.
stories_used: Record gas price surge fuels sharp rise in U.S. inflation - Honolulu Star-Advertiser
Sam Altman’s Home Attacked With Molotov Cocktail, Suspect Taken Into Custody - WSJ
What smart people are saying about Mythos, Anthropic's new AI model that has some cybersecurity experts spooked - Business Insider
The Latest Foldable iPhone Rumors: What's Changed and What We Know Now - MacRumors
